goal: Implement mainline v0.1 - distributed intent ledger for AI coding agents

summary.title: Implement mainline v0.1 — distributed intent ledger for AI coding agents
summary.what: Initial Go implementation of the mainline tool. Defines the domain layer (DraftIntent, IntentView, Turn, CommitNote, SemanticFingerprint, SealResult, MainlineView, ProposedIndex), the canonical-JSON / canonical-hash primitives, the state machine for intent lifecycle (drafting → sealed_local → proposed → merged → reverted, plus abandoned/superseded), and the engine surface (Init, Status, Start, Append, Show, Abandon, SealPrepare, SealSubmit, Publish, Merge, Sync, Reconcile, Check, ListProposals, Log, Context, Thread*). Uses git plumbing for the actor log (refs/heads/_mainline/actor/<actor_id>) and git notes (refs/notes/mainline/intents) as the source of truth for merged status (rc3 design). Ships a cobra-based CLI binding every engine method, a default agent adapter, AGENTS.md/PR-template generation in init, and a test suite covering core, engine, integration and (initial hand-rolled) property-based tests with benchmarks.
summary.why: MVP for the project: prove the spec is implementable in a single Go module that can self-bootstrap. The repo's own AGENTS.md mandates using mainline while modifying code; without a v0.1 binary that contract is unfulfillable. A working v0.1 also unblocks downstream work — every later intent (PBT expansion, schema evolution, conflict-check tuning) can record itself through the very tool it builds.
summary.user_goal: Implement mainline v0.1 — distributed intent ledger for AI coding agents

decisions.point: Source of truth for merged status
decisions.chose: git notes on main commits (rc3 patch)
decisions.rationale: Lets a clean squash commit message stay human-readable while still attaching machine-readable intent metadata. Survives rebases and squashes that would have mangled trailers.
rejected: Commit-message trailers (Mainline-Intent: ...) — earlier rc1/rc2 design, replaced because squash merges and rebase rewrites silently dropped them

decisions.chose: Append-only git refs under refs/heads/_mainline/actor/<actor_id>, each commit holding one event.json blob
decisions.rationale: Reuses git's content-addressable storage and remote sync machinery for free; multi-actor coordination is just `git fetch` of those refs.

decisions.chose: .ml-cache/ for drafts/views/identity (gitignored); .mainline/ for committed team config
decisions.rationale: Cleanly separates per-actor mutable state from team-shared configuration.

decisions.chose: Hand-rolled randomised loops on top of math/rand
decisions.rationale: No external dependency in v0.1; proves the engine satisfies basic invariants. Acknowledged limitation: no shrinking, fixed iteration counts. (Replaced in a follow-up intent that adopts pgregory.net/rapid.)

risks: scanMainNotes iterated commits newest-first without explicit chronological replay; revert commits stacked on top of merge commits could be masked. (Not caught by v0.1 tests; surfaced and fixed by the rapid PBT in the follow-up intent.)
Init does not commit .gitignore so a later `git add .` from an agent could accidentally pick up .ml-cache contents on a fresh clone.
followups: Replace hand-rolled PBT loops with a real framework (rapid) and add coverage for sync/reconcile idempotency, replay consistency, revert paths, and CommitNote schema compatibility.
Make `mainline init` commit its own .gitignore additions to avoid the agent footgun above.
Define a precedence policy if multiple notes ever target the same commit for the same intent (today the last writer wins via `git notes add -f`).
Add a `Reconcile` PBT proving that NotesAdd's force-overwrite cannot silently strip an unrelated intent already attached to the same commit.

architectural_claims: git notes (refs/notes/mainline/intents) are the source of truth for merged status; commit messages stay clean.

Per-actor append-only git refs (refs/heads/_mainline/actor/<actor_id>) carry sealed/abandoned/superseded events; `mainline sync` rebuilds the materialized MainlineView from those refs plus main-branch notes.
Engine is a single Service facade over Git + Store; CLI is a thin cobra wrapper that JSON-encodes engine results.
behavioral_changes: `mainline init` configures `notes.displayRef` and adds remote.origin.{fetch,push} refspecs for refs/notes/mainline/* so notes ride along with normal pushes/fetches when the remote already exists at init time.

data_model_changes.name: DraftIntent / IntentView / Turn / CommitNote / SealResult / MainlineView / ProposedIndex / SemanticFingerprint

security_implications: Identity is local-only (actor_<8 hex>) generated from crypto/rand; no signing of events in v0.1 — a malicious actor with write access to the remote could forge actor-log entries.
